definition: A cultivated perception that keeps the impermanent character of conditioned experience in view.
notes: Use this record for the three-marks practice family when impermanence is cultivated as a steady perception rather than treated as a mere abstract doctrine. In the canonical perception lists it belongs to the insight-training sequence and should stay linked to the broader progression toward seeing dissatisfaction and not-self.

example_phrases.notes: Listed among the cultivated perceptions used for insight and release.
